channel.title: Science & Nature News
channel.description: Latest news from BBC Sky at Night Magazine, BBC Science Focus, and Discover Wildlife

item.title: How to see the Lyrid meteor shower, spring's best chance to catch a shooting star
item.description: Our complete guide to seeing the Lyrids

item.title: Best things to see in the Southern Hemisphere sky, April 2026
item.description: Find out what's in the night sky tonight from your Southern Hemisphere location.

item.title: Best binocular targets to see tonight | Best things to see in the night sky with binoculars tonight
item.description: With binoculars you can see so much more in the night sky. Take our deep-sky tour and find out.

item.title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS spewed 70 swimming pools of water every day as it flew past the Sun
item.description: Water was escaping at an enormous rate as the interstellar comet flew close to the Sun

item.title: I'm going to put a hotel on the Moon, and I plan to do it by 2032. Here's how
item.description: Bored of the beach? Sick of city breaks? Step this way. Space entrepreneur Skyler Chan explains how he’ll build a holiday destination on the Moon by 2030

item.title: Did these historic NASA spacecraft find life on Mars – and accidentally kill it?
item.description: The Viking landers may have found organic molecules, but accidentally destroyed them

item.title: They're coming home. How to watch the Artemis II crew's splashdown live as they return to Earth tonight
item.description: Watch live as the Artemis II crew come home

item.title: Scientists thought there was a black hole at the centre of our Galaxy. It turns out it may be something even stranger
item.description: Could the 'black hole' at the centre of our Galaxy actually be dark matter?

item.title: "Wanted to say a couple of things to my astro sister" The Artemis II crew just spoke to astronauts on board the Space Station
item.description: The Artemis II crew and the International Space Station astronauts held a brief call during which they spoke with one another.

item.title: The Artemis II crew saw a unique type of solar eclipse visible only to them – and Venus photobombed
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts photographed their very own solar eclipse

item.title: Saturn’s moon Titan may hold the building blocks of alien life in its methane lakes and rivers
item.description: Study suggests that life’s chemical precursors could arise in non-water environments

item.title: The cosmic explosion that wouldn't quit. Strange signal from deep space baffles astronomers
A real-life Tatooine? Astronomers discover trio of planets in rare double-sun system
item.description: Get the key facts on this story with our quick, 1-minute read Star system: astronomers have discovered three Earth-sized planets orbiting a binary star system known as TOI-2267 Close proximity: the system is located 190 lightyears from Earth, which is considered relatively nearby in astronomical terms Binary nature: the two stars in the system are cool and orbit each other at a distance just eight times the distance between Earth and the Sun Record breakers: this discovery represents the coldest and most compact pair of stars known to host planets Transit first: the study marks the first time planets have

item.title: A mega-building in China is now so large that it's slowing Earth's spin
item.description: It's even changing how long a day is

item.title: We’re losing 338 spoken words every day
item.description: We've been nattering less and less each year since 2005

item.title: 6 mind-blowing space missions now set to launch after Artemis II
item.description: From water hunting on the Moon to the closest planet to the Sun

item.title: Artemis II in pictures: 21 jaw-dropping photos, from launch to splashdown
item.description: Ten days, one Moon, and a view we’ve waited half a century to see again

item.title: What your vitamin D levels could reveal about your dementia risk
item.description: Scientists have found that people with higher vitamin D in midlife had less of a key Alzheimer’s protein in their brains years later

item.title: A massive once-in-500-years chimpanzee civil war has broken out
item.description: The largest-known group of wild chimpanzees has split in two. The aftermath has been brutal

item.title: How some people sleep only four hours a night and still feel great
item.description: A small group of humans barely rest at night – and never suffer for it. Science is about to find out why that matters for all of us

item.title: The unsettling truth about medical cannabis and mental health
item.description: Does it help with mental health, or is there something else at play?

item.title: Your reality really could be a simulation, say experts. Here’s why
item.description: As computers grow ever more powerful, the idea we could be living in a simulation is looking less like science fiction every day

item.title: The US is now paying more than any other country for climate change damage, study suggests
item.description: Despite being the biggest carbon emitter, the US is already paying a disproportionate price for the climate crisis

item.title: The 5 biggest myths about taking statins to lower cholesterol
item.description: Statins save lives – so why are so many people wary of taking them?

item.title: I'm a neuroscientist. Here's all you need to know about money to be truly happy
item.description: We all need enough funds to cover our basic needs, but beyond that the connection between wealth and wellness is less clear

item.title: There's no such thing as a shark, suggest scientists
item.description: Analysis of shark DNA has thrown a wrench into our understanding of these sharp-toothed predators

item.title: We’re closer than ever to bringing back life from cryogenic freezing
item.description: Astronauts in cryosleep is one of science fiction's most enduring fantasies – but a new study suggests the gap between fiction and reality just got a little smaller

item.title: An ancient Maya ‘codex’ keeps predicting future events with eerie accuracy
item.description: The Maya civilisation is known for its art and architecture. But as we decipher their surviving cultural relics, we’re discovering Maya astronomy was even more advanced than we had imagined

item.title: The unsettling reasons bowel cancer might be rising among under-50s
item.description: Deaths from the main types of cancer in people under 50 are falling. But for bowel cancer, they’re increasing

item.title: This (very weird) rule has been controlling fashion for 150 years
item.description: Ever wondered why low-rise jeans are making a comeback?

item.title: Scientists want to create ‘T. Rex’ quakes on the Moon
item.description: Artificial Moonquakes on demand could help scientists investigate below the lunar surface

item.title: Your most draining relationships (except one) are taking years off your life, study suggests
item.description: Difficult people don’t just zap your energy – they may also accelerate your biological ageing

item.title: We've found a bizarre alien magma planet surprisingly close to Earth
item.description: The floor (and the rest of this planet) is lava

item.title: Just one day in your life could soon reveal how long you’ll live
item.description: After building a Truman Show experiment, scientists were able to predict the lifespans of their 81 subjects

item.title: This stunning 3D map lets anyone explore real human organs down to a single cell
item.description: It’s far less gross than it sounds (we promise) and could have major implications for how we understand anatomy and disease

item.title: This terrifying new dinosaur could rewrite the origins of T. rex
item.description: The emergence of the world’s most iconic predator remains a scientific mystery. This latest discovery offers a critical clue

item.title: ‘Stunning’ new treatment may reverse frailty after just one dose
item.description: A landmark trial suggests stem cells can reverse the physical decline of ageing

item.title: Thylacine paintings discovered in Australian cave less than 1,000 years old, say scientists. Here's why that's a big deal
item.description: The carnivorous marsupial is widely thought to have gone extinct on mainland Australia about 3,000 years ago. Artworks found recently in Northern Territory suggests otherwise.

item.title: In a remote Himalayan valley, 3 apex predators live peacefully side by side. Camera traps just revealed how they do it
item.description: Leopards, snow leopards and wolves have found a way to live in relative peace, according to a new study.

item.title: This curious creature has a fishing rod dangling out of its head. It doesn't just use it for hunting…
item.description: Researchers studied more than 100 species of anglerfish to learn more about the secrets of their legendary lures.

item.title: Scientists drop cameras 9,100m deep off coast of Japan. What they find baffles them
item.description: While exploring deep-sea trenches off Japan, researchers found a creature so weird they couldn’t identify it.

item.title: Why San Francisco Bay has suddenly become a death trap for gray whales
item.description: Climate change is pushing starving gray whales into California's San Francisco Bay, where they are often hit and killed by boats, according to a new study.

item.title: Hyena with zebra head in jaws among 14 powerful images from close-up photo challenge
item.description: See striking photos from Close-up Photographers of the Year’s ‘Death & Decay’ challenge

item.title: Emperor penguin and Antarctic fur seal declared Endangered
item.description: Climate change is pushing these Antarctic species to the brink

item.title: Largest known wild chimp group splits in two – sparking violence and death
item.description: Uganda's Ngogo chimps have broken into two permanent groups, resulting in the killing of numerous adults and infants, say researchers.

item.title: 500-million-year-old clawed predator found in Utah desert forces scientists to rethink origin of spiders
item.description: Named after French explorer Jacques Cousteau, Megachelicerax cousteaui is the oldest of its kind ever found, not to mention one of the strangest…

item.title: Satellite images reveal hidden cause of melting glaciers in Greenland
item.description: A new study has found that meltwater lakes at the edge of the Greenland ice sheet are accelerating the flow of major glaciers, potentially contributing to global sea level rise.

item.title: “It was just bones all over the floor.” Fossil hunters make astounding discovery in Texan water cave
item.description: A flooded cave in central Texas has yielded the remains of Ice Age animals that have never been found in the region before…

item.title: When 30 tiny concrete 'apartments' popped up in an Australian rainforest, a bunch of elusive animals moved in
item.description: Scientists built tiny shelters for ground-dwelling frogs in Queensland to see which ones they liked the best. Here's what they discovered...

item.title: Crab wanders through Suez Canal into Mediterranean Sea. Now scientists are worried
item.description: The portunid crab is native to the Red Sea and the Indian Ocean, but now it’s being found in the Mediterranean. Here's why that could be a problem.

item.title: For 50 years, there have been rumours of these fish climbing waterfalls. Scientists just filmed them doing it for the first time
item.description: Remarkable footage shows hundreds of shellear fish scrambling up Luvilombo Falls in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

item.title: Researchers tracked 9 great hammerhead sharks off Florida and the Bahamas. Here's what they discovered
item.description: Scientists wanted to learn more about how the largest hammerhead species on the planet may cope as the climate changes.

item.title: Scientists looked inside a bee hive. What they saw is remarkable
item.description: Researchers wanted to learn more about the insect's famous 'waggle dance'. Here's what they found.

item.title: Great crested grebe seen drowning and eating chiffchaffs in Spain
item.description: Birdwatcher and photographer Juan Montiel captured the unusual grebe behaviour on camera.

item.title: This baby elephant taking its first trembling steps is the cutest thing you’ll see today
item.description: Baby elephants learn to walk within the first hour of their life. If they didn’t, they might not survive.

item.title: There's a tropical island in the middle of the Panama Canal that's home to a bright pink animal with an astonishing trick
item.description: Researchers on Barro Colorado Island say the animal changes colour from pink to green. And they think they know why

item.title: "Exceptional discovery." Animals thought extinct for 6,000 years found alive in Indonesian rainforest
item.description: Two marsupial species previously only known from the fossil record have been rediscovered in New Guinea.

item.title: “It’s a bit like sea turtle forensics." Scientists looked inside 24 turtles – and uncovered secrets from the past
item.description: Sea turtle shells are helping researchers learn more about major environmental changes in the ocean's past.

item.title: Strange organism that looks like a tongue found in English woodland. It has never been seen before in the UK
item.description: The rare blue-based earthtongue fungus was discovered at Kingley Vale National Nature Reserve in West Sussex.

item.title: Scientists tracked 39 polar bears and 26 seals through the Arctic wilderness. What they discovered is important
item.description: The researchers wanted to learn more about how climate change is reshaping Arctic food webs.

item.title: Biologists stuck a camera on a sea turtle – and it filmed this
item.description: TurtleCams are helping marine researchers learn more about the lives of sea turtles as they cruise through our seas.
